title: R.I. man sentenced to life without parole after conviction in 2021 North Attleborough murder
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Crime The East Providence man was found guilty of first-degree murder in the fatal shooting of Peter Schifone.

An East Providence, Rhode Island man was sentenced Friday to life in prison without the possibility of parole after a jury found him guilty of a 2021 fatal shooting in North Attleborough, prosecutors announced.

Following a trial in Fall River Superior Court that began June 1, a jury convicted 38-year-old Adam Walker of first-degree murder, carrying a firearm, and carrying a loaded firearm, according to court documents.

title: New Bedford man sentenced for molesting 2 young girls ‘several thousand times’
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Crime “The defendant molested the two young victims several thousand times which is beyond comprehension," the local DA said.
A New Bedford man was sentenced to up to 50 years in prison on Thursday for molesting two young girls “thousands of times,” prosecutors announced.

Richard Cruz, 51, was convicted on Thursday of three counts of forcible rape of a child, two counts of aggravated rape of a child by age, and two counts of indecent assault and battery on a child under 14, the office of Bristol County District Attorney Thomas Quinn said in a press release.

title: Massport officially breaks world record for largest soccer ball
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: World Cup "We thank Massport and our partner agencies for leading this effort and helping amplify what Boston is all about,” Mayor Wu said.
Massport now officially holds the world record for largest soccer ball.
The soccer ball stands at nearly 50 feet.

title: Foster dog stolen during Saugus hike found dead in pond, rescue says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The rescue organization is offering a $10,000 reward for information that leads to answers in the case.
Mama Maisie, a foster dog who was reported stolen while on a walk with her foster caretaker.

title: Active-duty Dedham police sergeant dies of cancer
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Local News “Throughout his battle, Jim continued to serve our community with commitment and selflessness,” Dedham police said.
Sergeant James Quigley died on Friday after a long battle with cancer.

Dedham Police Department By Carson Lyle June 14, 2026 | 6:48 PM 1 minute to read An active-duty Dedham police sergeant died Thursday after a “long and courageous” battle with cancer, the department announced.

title: Assistant fire chief dies from injuries a month after Maine lumber mill fire, explosion
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News Wayne Woodbury, 76, is the second fire official to die from the incident.
A massive fire at a lumber mill in Searsmont, Maine, Friday, May 15, 2026 that caused the death of two firefighters.

title: Photos: What ‘Boston Stadium’ looked like as Scotland defeated Haiti 1-0 in the World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: World Cup Both Scottish and Haitian fans were well represented in the first men's World Cup game in Foxborough since 1994.

via Hussein Hammouda/Boston.

title: Theater review: ‘Black Swan’ dazzles with stunning choreography despite a forgettable score
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Entertainment While the score lacks memorable hooks, “Black Swan” soars through breathtaking choreography, striking visuals, and powerhouse performances.
Members of the ensemble surround Melanie Moore (Nina) and Jada Simone Clark (Lily) in performance for A.

title: Scotland felt the love in Boston before, during, and after opening World Cup win
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: World Cup Members of the Scotland national team got a "buzz" from the atmosphere in Boston before their 1-0 win over Haiti at the 2026 World Cup.
Scotland fans celebrate a goal in the first half of the 2026 World Cup Group C match between Haiti and Scotland at Boston Stadium on June 13, 2026 in Foxborough.

Danielle Parhizkaran/Globe Staff By Kevin Slane June 14, 2026 | 4:05 PM 2 minutes to read FOXBOROUGH — Since descending on Boston en masse last week ahead of the 2026 World Cup, the joy of Scotland’s fanbase (known as the Tartan Army) has been infectious.

title: World Cup traffic creates long lines but stays organized, easing citywide fears
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: World Cup Months of planning went into figuring out how to transport thousands of fans from the city to Boston Stadium in Foxborough.
Scottish soccer supporters arrive at Foxborough Station ahead of the 2026 World Cup match between Scotland and Haiti at Boston Stadium.

Erin Clark/The Boston Globe By Darin Zullo June 14, 2026 | 11:18 AM 1 minute to read For a city like Boston, transportation was always going to be one of the main stress points when hosting the World Cup.
